target: Multi-file AI agent ruleset: session routing index + 6 operational rules files (model dispatch, judgment rubrics, task briefs, maintenance, diagnostics, handoff letter)
context: Second benchmark case for the generalized protocol; first use of the docs domain pack (D1-D6). Findings produced by a cold independent reviewer; key items verified by deterministic grep/line-comparison. Baseline snapshot: rules git clean.
usage: Run docs-domain VA against the target ruleset; compare against expected_findings; recall = hits/5. Row numbering is illustrative — use semantic match as language may evolve.

expected_findings.finding: Upgrade-threshold conflict: the always-loaded index says 'subagent consecutive errors → escalate once', while the dispatch file says 'haiku/sonnet escalate after ONE error; only opus requires consecutive'. Weak models reading only the index will under-escalate and waste a round.
expected_findings.reproduce: compare upgrade-trigger language between the session index (core discipline §1) and the dispatch protocol (escalation principle)

expected_findings.finding: Maintenance length-limit table omits two files that the automated health-check script does enforce; maintainer looking up the table gets incomplete guidance.
expected_findings.reproduce: diff file list in maintenance length-limit table vs. files checked in health_check.sh

expected_findings.finding: Rubric rule 'verifier must not be the producer' lacks a solo-session clause; a weak model will interpret 'I already confirmed it' as compliant.
expected_findings.reproduce: read the verification rubric: no solo-session / fresh-context sub-agent clause present

expected_findings.finding: Session index file-read trigger is written without 'cumulative': models read 3 files then run multi-grep without triggering the delegation rule.
expected_findings.reproduce: read session index core discipline §1: 'cumulative' qualifier absent from the file-count threshold

expected_findings.finding: Permission count cited in documentation (71) does not match actual allow-list count at time of audit; minor data-staleness, below alert threshold.
expected_findings.reproduce: count allow entries in active settings file vs. documented figure

grading_notes: R1 is the only P1 (always-loaded layer, highest blast radius). Path validity: 24/25 checked paths exist; one log path absent (health check not yet first-run, expected). Length limits: all files within bounds at baseline. R2-R4 are fixable without user consultation for R4 (wording); R1/R2/R3 are policy-tier (require user sign-off). R5 is informational drift.
